items.correct: The combined loss of water from a surface through evaporation (from soil and water bodies) and transpiration (from plant leaves).
items.misconception: It is only water evaporating from puddles and lakes.
items.random_wrong: It is the process of rain falling from clouds.
items.why: Students focus only on evaporation. Transpiration from forests can move enormous quantities of water — a key concept for understanding deforestation's effect on the water cycle.

items.q: Why is a food web a better model than a food chain?
items.correct: A food chain shows one linear path; a food web shows the many interconnected feeding relationships in reality, so it better represents how species depend on multiple food sources.
items.misconception: A food web is the same as a food chain, just drawn differently.
items.random_wrong: A food web shows what spiders eat and a food chain shows what other animals eat.
items.why: Most species have multiple predators and prey. A linear chain is a simplification — the web captures redundancy and resilience. Removing one species rarely collapses a real ecosystem.

items.q: What is the difference between biotic and abiotic components of an ecosystem?
items.correct: Biotic components are all living organisms (plants, animals, microbes); abiotic components are non-living factors (climate, soil, water, light, rock).
items.misconception: Biotic means animals and abiotic means plants.
items.random_wrong: Biotic and abiotic both refer to types of soil.
items.why: Plants are biotic, not abiotic. Students sort by familiarity rather than living vs non-living. The distinction matters because abiotic factors set the conditions for which biotic communities develop.

items.q: Kielder Water is which type of resource?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Reservoir. “Desalination plant” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: Salt. “Microplastics” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Capacity of Kielder Water in billions of litres?
items.why: The real figure is 200. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
